Alright, so focus on obtaining Relics that can raise your overall Damage Output such as Black Star Ember, Hierophant's Staff, Omen Ritual Bird, Salvific Limb. Alternatively, you should also look into picking up Items that can raise your overall Sustainability / Survivability such as Lamprey's Kiss, Our Home, Preserved Butterfly and Safe Passage; I personally wasn't able to obtain them, so I instead picked up Harford's Elixir and then picked up Symptoms on purpose through the Blood Pools to raise my Maximum Health. You should also go into the Stage, expecting to consume at least one Emergency Gnosis so I equipped Dusk & Dawn and Hope on one of my Supports.

In the Boss Battle, don't aim for a big burst turn at the beginning of the Stage as he'll be obtaining lots of Fortress on top of all his Revives so you should instead open each turn with Mouchette's Exalt followed by your Strikes to slowly chip away at their lives and health. And due to the nature of this being a long Stage, you should aim to Rouse at least 2 or more of your Characters. I also ended up swapping Aigis for Pickman on my end so that I can have somebody to trigger the Chains of Resentment on the Backline of the Mob Waves & on the Final Boss's last life. || Over here, I could've ended the Battle much sooner but I held onto the last unique Card the Boss gave me and ended up wasting more Turns for no reason. ||
Looked at it and I don’t know how I’d be able to do it w/ only two accounts? Maybe three?
Is there like a specific stage that works as a benchmark for whether a team “works” or we really just gotta drop the teams into multiple rando stages to test
with some experience you can vibe it
the 4 things u need in a team are dps, vulnerable, keyflare, and if you arent one shotting everything, weakness/defensive support
so if a team lacks one of these its going to be bad
but the best way is just testing

Let's say you want 4 accounts for the skin.
1. Install LDPlayer and download Morimens but don't open the game yet.
2. Open LDMultiplayer and clone 3 instances.
3. Play all of them to 1-5, and use all stamina on any interlude daily. Don't pull with silver, use them for stamina refill.
You can run multiple instances at the same time if your pc can handle it.

ok so catena play pattern is 
play crimson schakles 3x to convert them into flails (4 cost), then u need to save up multiple flails 
on the burst turn (turn u want to kill) you use her exalt then dump all your flails, each flail makes the next flail do more dmg
thats why u need to save them up and not use them without a plan
Very probably Hamelyn, 2 other units are complete unknowns

No problem man, I'm happy to help and answer questions like these as they rarely ever come by and feel like they should be given more attention as there are people who genuinely struggle with stations like these and have no idea what they could've done instead to maybe ease the process of it all. Yeah, those Relics definitely help alot with your likelihood of not immediately dying due to the scaling nature of the boss that it makes up much more difficult to defeat.

Fsfs, it's a real pain to me and I personally spent roughly three or four straight hours stressing when I had first encountered the boss and wondered what I could've done differently to overcome everything and against all odds eventually clear it. On the Caro / Chaos side of things you could probably use Lotan (AoE Skills, comes with Strikes but will share Arithmatecia resources with Mouchette) or Leigh (can recover based on lost Health via Exalt with permanent Strength coming as a nice bonus, 3-Cost is a Triple Hit that pierced enemy behind initial Target but is random and has an 0-Cost that trades your Health for more temporary Strength)
